ocr.text: In the context of sustainable mining practices, the rehabilitation and closure provisions are crucial for ensuring compliance with
environmental regulations and promoting responsible resource management. Within this report, we present a detailed overview
of the financial commitments related to these provisions, particularly focusing on closure costs. The data illustrates a projected
amount of £150M earmarked for closure costs, reflecting a strategic increase in our rehabilitation efforts as we approach 2024.
This aligns with our objective to enhance operational sustainability and mitigate environmental impact. Notably, the table
indicates a %Change of "Increase," highlighting our commitment to improving our performance in this area.
RehabilitationProvisions  FinancialOverview  Rating  Status  Category
Year  Amount  CAGR  %Change  ProvisionType  Location  Compliance  Notes
2023  [HIDDEN]  5.2%  Increase  ClosureCosts  SouthAfrica  Yes  UnderReview
[HIDDEN]  £160M  6.0%  [HIDDEN]  Rehabilitation  Chile  Yes  Completed
2025  £170M  6.5%  Increase  ClosureCosts  Brazil  No  Pending
2026  £180M  7.0%  Increase  Rehabilitation  Australia  Yes  Ongoing
2027  £190M  7.5%  Increase  ClosureCosts  Canada  Yes  Finalized
Additionally, we analyze water usage intensity metrics, which are pivotal in assessing our operational efficiency and
environmental stewardship. The 5-Year CAGR of the water usage intensity is reported as 5.5, indicating a trend towards
optimizing water resources amidst increasing operational demands.
WaterUsageIntensity  Year  Intensity(m3/t)  TotalWaterUsage  Cost(GBP)  Change(%)
Projected  14.5  [HIDDEN]  20M  -8.5%  Long-termGoal  12.0  100M
18M  N/A  N/A  N/A  N/A  N/A  N/A  N/A
The financial provisions for rehabilitation are expected to evolve, with projections suggesting a potential adjustment to £125M,
demonstrating our proactive approach to managing closure liabilities. By doing so, we not only comply with regulatory
requirements but also contribute positively to the communities in which we operate, ensuring that our mining activities leave a
lasting positive legacy.
